message:
test(lifecycle): diag reports zero sizes for absent db+index (ibuuh.19)

ibuuh.19 retention-invariant row: 'derivative asset retention,
quarantine inspection, garbage collection'. When cass diag --json
reports database/index as absent on a fresh isolated HOME, their
size_bytes MUST be 0 — and conversations/messages counts must also be
zero rather than inheriting stale cached values. A retention bug
where cached sizes leak into a fresh HOME would silently misreport
disk usage to operators; this test pins the 'clean slate = zero
bytes' invariant so the regression fails CI.

New test diag_reports_zero_sizes_for_absent_db_and_index asserts:
 * database.exists = false AND database.size_bytes = 0
 * index.exists = false AND index.size_bytes = 0
 * database.conversations = 0
 * database.messages = 0

VERIFICATION:
cargo test --test lifecycle_matrix: 63/63 pass.

Follow-up slice on coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.19; bead stays
open for the quarantine/GC half that needs upstream cleanup and
retention subsystems from in-flight work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
